title: LCL Sea Is Incidental
wikitext:
[]: LCL Sea Is Incidental             Quote Post by Bentkey » Sun May 12, 2013 8:48 pm It would seem to me that the actual sea of LCL is not the unified consciousness that Seele was after. I think the unified consciousness is Rei/lillith's soul ball as it contains, well, everyone's souls. I think the LCL that is left behind is overall unimportant. Also the sea of LCL couldn't house a unified conscious because it's not unified. Some people like Keel and Gendo are completely alone and leave behind a small, lonely puddle, while other people's puddles do manage to mix into a larger mass. So I think that the conversations Shinji has with humanity in episodes 25- 26 happen within Lilith's soul ball. When Shinji realizes the importance of individuality his soul is returned to the LCL which manifests into himself. Feel free to point out any errors in my post.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon May 13, 2013 3:59 am The LCL puddle that people turn into are certainly not the seas of LCL. These explosion seem to be a simple byproduct of the forced release of the individual's soul so that it can be gathered into Lilith's egg. The puddle are indeed most likely unimportant. The Sea of LCL is conjectured to be essentially an infinite other universe, accessed via Eva and Angel cores. It is further assumed to be the source or fount of souls. The ability of beings to form out of LCL is at least suggested by Nerv attempt to salvage Shinji in episode #20. However, there is evidence that Cores are filled with LCL, so that Shinji's reconstitution out of Eva-01's core could also suggest this.
